name: Grilled Venison Tenderloin
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds venison tenderloin, cut into 1/2 inch filets
recipeInstructions:
1. Ask your butcher or game processor to remove the fat and silver skin (white muscle fiber) from the venison tenderloin to reduce the gamy taste.
2. For the marinade, mix all wet and dry ingredients until blended in a shallow non-reactive dish.
3. Place the filets into the dish, pouring the marinade over them. Cover and refrigerate for 72 hours, turning the meat over once a day.
4. Remove the filets from the dish and pat them dry with paper towels before grilling. Reserve the remaining marinade for basting sauce.
5. Prepare charcoal in a pyramid shape and light using lighter fluid, an electric starter, or a chimney starter. Wait until the coals are gray and spread them in a single layer for direct heat.
6. Spray the grill grid with non-stick spray to ensure it heats evenly over the hot coals (the hotter the grid, the less chance of food sticking).
7. Place the filets on the grid. Grill for 6-7 minutes, basting with sauce and turning once, then grill an additional 5-6 minutes, basting again until reaching desired doneness (medium rare is recommended; cooking time should be under 13 minutes if preferred rare or medium well).
8. Baste frequently as venison is very lean and requires careful attention to prevent toughness.
